etymology.description: 矢(矢) (shǐ, "arrow") + 委(委) (wěi, "entrust") = semantic arrow and phonetic component -> short stature
etymology.interpretation: 矮(矮) (ǎi, "short") combines 矢(矢) (shǐ, "arrow") as the semantic component related to precision or directness with 委(委) (wěi, "entrust") providing the phonetic cue, together conveying the idea of shortness or low stature.

examples.chinese: 房子的天花板很矮(房子的天花板很矮)。
examples.english: The ceiling of the house is very low.

examples.chinese: 在篮球队中(在籃球隊中)，他显得特别矮(他顯得特別矮)。
examples.pinyin: Zài lánqiú duì zhōng, tā xiǎnde tèbié ǎi.
examples.english: In the basketball team, he appears particularly short.
